title: Taxonomy Cross-Reference Analysis Results

well_represented.insight: Simpsons excels at STRUCTURAL patterns - callbacks, timing, character archetypes

simpsons_coverage.insight: Simpsons lacks SHORT-FORM patterns - wordplay, social scripts, POV tricks

well_represented.insight: TikTok excels at SOCIAL patterns - recognition humor, in-group bonding, workplace reality

tiktok_coverage.insight: TikTok lacks COMPLEX STRUCTURE patterns - these require longer format

callback_payoff.description: Marge's bowling ball always hits exactly pin 7
callback_payoff.structural_formula: PLANT specific detail → BUILD expectation → RETURN with confirmation

non_reaction_deadpan.description: Homer sees kitchen chaos, pauses, walks out
non_reaction_deadpan.structural_formula: ABSURD situation → EXPECTED reaction → NO reaction (contrast)

lampshading.description: Hank Scorpio breaks fourth wall with knowing look
lampshading.structural_formula: ABSURD element → CHARACTER acknowledges → CONTINUES anyway

escalation_subversion.description: Bart passes dangerous objects, picks Magic 8-Ball as weapon
escalation_subversion.structural_formula: BUILD escalation → EXPECT continuation → ANTICLIMACTIC choice

tone_dependent_interpretation.description: Same structure reads differently based on acting/dialogue tone

cinematic_interiority.description: POV + sound perspective implies internal thoughts without narration

social_invitation_violation.description: Breaking implicit contracts within social rituals

empathetic_humor.description: Humor through viewer sympathy, not just surprise

coherent_absurdist_world.description: World-building where different rules apply consistently

priority_1_simpsons_mining.reason: Rich structural examples that teach HOW patterns work

priority_2_tiktok_relabeling.reason: Many examples exist but labeled with generic mechanisms

priority_3_gap_filling.reason: Sparse in both datasets - fundamental comedy structures
priority_3_gap_filling.action: Create synthetic examples or find external sources

key_insight: Simpsons teaches STRUCTURAL MECHANICS (timing, callbacks, escalation). TikTok teaches SOCIAL RECOGNITION (workplace reality, in-group bonding). Combined training creates model that understands both HOW patterns work and WHAT they look like in modern short-form.
